3 min read
4 hours ago

--

The React ecosystem evolves incredibly fast.
Every year, new frameworks, libraries, and developer tools promise to make us more productive.
Yet one surprisingly common task still feels unnecessarily slow:
Previewing a JSX file.
Whether the component comes from an AI assistant, a teammate, or your own project, the process usually looks the same.
Open a React project.
Copy the component.
Install dependencies.
Run the development server.
Wait.
All of this just to answer one question:
“How does it look?”
The Workflow That Slows Everyone Down
Modern AI tools can generate an entire React component in seconds.
Ironically, previewing that component often takes longer than generating it.
If you work with ChatGPT, Claude, Cursor, Bolt, or Lovable, you’ve probably previewed dozens — maybe hundreds — of AI-generated components.
Each one requires the same repetitive setup.
The process works.
But it isn’t efficient.
The Missing Piece
Imagine receiving a JSX file from a colleague.
Instead of creating a temporary project, you simply double-click the file.
A window opens.
The component renders instantly.
That’s it.
No terminal.
No configuration.
No unnecessary steps.
That simple experience became the motivation behind PreviewKit.
Built Around One Simple Goal
PreviewKit isn’t trying to replace your favorite development tools.
You’ll still use React.
You’ll still use Vite or Next.js.
You’ll still write code in VS Code.
PreviewKit simply removes one repetitive task from your workflow:
Previewing JSX components quickly.
Sometimes the most valuable developer tools aren’t the ones with the longest feature list.
They’re the ones that eliminate everyday friction.
Why This Matters More Than Ever
AI has fundamentally changed frontend development.
Developers are no longer writing every component manually.
Instead, they’re reviewing, refining, and improving AI-generated code.
That shift makes fast visual feedback more important than ever.
The quicker you can preview a component, the quicker you can iterate.
And faster iteration usually leads to better software.
Designed for Developers
PreviewKit is especially useful for developers who:

• Build React applications daily

• Generate UI with AI coding assistants

• Review components from teammates

• Prototype interfaces quickly

• Experiment with different designs
Instead of spending time preparing a preview environment, you can focus on evaluating the component itself.
Productivity Isn’t About Working Faster
Many people think productivity means typing code faster.
In reality, productivity often comes from removing unnecessary steps.
Opening fewer projects.
Running fewer commands.
Switching between fewer tools.
Those small improvements compound over time.
PreviewKit was built around that philosophy.
Not by adding more features.
But by removing unnecessary work.
Final Thoughts
Technology keeps getting faster.
Our workflows should too.
If AI can generate a React component in a few seconds, previewing that component shouldn’t take several minutes.
PreviewKit exists to bridge that gap.
Because developers should spend their time building great software — not setting up temporary projects just to preview a single JSX file.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Less Setup. More Building.
